description: Canonical event vocabulary for Epic 5 music-event inference and review.

categories.name: transition
categories.description: Structural shifts that change energy direction or listener expectation.

categories.name: drop_variant
categories.description: Release-phase event types that refine the parent drop concept.

categories.name: sustained_state
categories.description: Persistent song states that describe plateau, loop, or held-tension behavior.

categories.name: vocal_phrase
categories.description: Vocal-led moments that often need phrase-aware lighting treatment.

categories.name: arrangement_change
categories.description: Layering changes that reshape density without necessarily creating a full transition.

categories.name: accent
categories.description: Short impact-style events that mark emphasis inside a larger section or phrase.

events.name: build
events.description: Escalating pre-release energy with rising density, tension, or expectation.

events.name: breakdown
events.description: A deliberate reduction in arrangement, density, or intensity before a later change.

events.name: drop
events.description: The main tension-release event where built energy resolves into peak motion or impact.

events.name: drop_explode
events.description: A drop variant with explosive onset, large spectral expansion, and obvious impact emphasis.

events.name: drop_groove
events.description: A groove-led drop with sustained rhythmic drive rather than a single oversized hit.

events.name: drop_punch
events.description: A compact, punch-forward drop that emphasizes transient impact over wide release bloom.

events.name: soft_release
events.description: A release event that resolves tension with a smoother or less aggressive energy landing.

events.name: no_drop_plateau
events.description: A plateau after expectation-building where no full drop materializes.

events.name: fake_drop
events.description: A fake-out release cue that withholds the expected drop payoff.

events.name: tension_hold
events.description: A held state where tension persists without resolving into a drop or reset.

events.name: pause_break
events.description: A stop-time, pause, or near-silent interruption used as a structural punctuation point.

events.name: anthem_call
events.description: A strong lead-vocal call moment meant to frame a crowd-facing or singalong phrase.

events.name: call_response
events.description: A phrase pair where one motif is answered by another voice, line, or instrumental phrase.

events.name: hook_phrase
events.description: A recurring hook or refrain phrase that acts as a memorable focal line.

events.name: vocal_spotlight
events.description: A voice-led window where the vocal envelope dominates the listener focus over the supporting arrangement.

events.name: vocal_tail
events.description: A trailing vocal decay or handoff where the vocal energy falls away while the section continues underneath.

events.name: energy_reset
events.description: A clear reset of intensity or momentum before the next section re-establishes direction.

events.name: layer_add
events.description: An arrangement moment where material is added and density or width meaningfully increases.

events.name: layer_remove
events.description: An arrangement moment where material is stripped back and density meaningfully decreases.

events.name: impact_hit
events.description: A short impact marker with strong transient emphasis or downbeat punctuation.

events.name: stinger
events.description: A compact one-shot accent or punctuation phrase that ends or punctuates a moment.

events.name: groove_loop
events.description: A sustained rhythmic loop state with stable pulse and repetition-driven momentum.

events.name: atmospheric_plateau
events.description: A persistent spacious or ambient plateau with low-volatility energy and texture emphasis.

events.name: percussion_break
events.description: A percussion-dominant pocket where drums carry the motion while harmonic and vocal material pull back.

events.name: instrumental_bed
events.description: A support-driven instrumental passage where accompaniment or texture carries the section more than the lead vocal.
